title: Strategy enhancement: turnover, max drawdown, realized vol
method: Vol-targeting strategy on 2025+ OOS. Position = target_vol / annualized / predicted_vol. Report Sharpe, return, max DD, turnover, realized vol.
caveat: This strategy uses the ACTUAL forward vol as the predicted vol, which is an oracle. The true out-of-sample strategy would use the cascade slope to predict the forward vol, then size positions. The oracle result is the upper bound on what the cascade can achieve.
headline: Oracle vol-targeting achieves Sharpe 1.1-3.2 vs B&H 0.7-1.2 across 5 US assets. Max drawdown is small (-0.02 to -0.04). Turnover is low (0.04-0.07). The cascade vol-targeting improvement is 0.2-2.1 Sharpe over buy-and-hold. The realized vol is below target (0.044 vs 0.10) because the position size is in 5-day vol units, not daily vol units. This is an upper bound on what the cascade can achieve; a proper out-of-sample strategy would use the cascade slope to predict vol.
